title: <div>  •  Haptics: Leverage Core Haptics (iOS) and Vibration API (Android) for s
content:
• Haptics: Leverage Core Haptics (iOS) and Vibration API (Android) for subtle feedback on dice rolls and piece placement. Ensure toggles for haptic intensity in settings.
• AR Mode: Use AR Foundation for optional AR board projection. Provide a fallback 3D mode with identical mechanics to ensure accessibility on non-AR-capable devices.
• Accessibility: Integrate VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) for voice commands. Use high-contrast textures and color-blind-friendly palettes (e.g., blue/orange over red/green). Test with axe-core for ARIA compliance.
Optimization:
• Use low-poly models and baked lighting for mid-tier devices (e.g., iPhone SE, mid-range Androids).
• Implement LOD (Level of Detail) for 3D assets to reduce rendering load.
• Cap frame rate at 60 FPS and optimize shaders for mobile GPUs (e.g., Adreno, Mali).
Challenges to Mitigate:
• Ensure gesture controls are intuitive by playtesting with diverse users (novices to experts).
• Provide an optional 2D board view for users with low-end devices or accessibility needs.
2. Thematic Environments & Historical Immersion
